scenario.title: Scheduling with mild interruption
scenario.goal: Schedule a new patient appointment while interrupting once or twice naturally if the office gives long explanations.
must_try: If the office gives a long explanation, cut in once with a direct scheduling question.
Keep the interruption natural and brief.
scenario.success_condition: The conversation remains coherent and the office recovers from interruption without losing the task.
good_bug_signals: The agent restarts or loops after interruption.
The agent ignores the interruption and continues off-topic.
The agent becomes incoherent after turn-taking overlap.

retell_llm_dynamic_variables.success_condition: The conversation remains coherent and the office recovers from interruption without losing the task.
retell_llm_dynamic_variables.goal: Schedule a new patient appointment while interrupting once or twice naturally if the office gives long explanations.
retell_llm_dynamic_variables.must_try: If the office gives a long explanation, cut in once with a direct scheduling question. | Keep the interruption natural and brief.
retell_llm_dynamic_variables.title: Scheduling with mild interruption

call_data.transcript:
User: This 
Agent: Hi, 
User: call may be recorded for quality and training purposes. 
Agent: That's 
User: Thanks 
Agent: fine, thank 
User: for calling Pivot Point Orthopaedics. Part of Pretty Good AI. Am I speaking with Maria? 
Agent: Yes, this is Maria Lopez. I’m calling to schedule a new patient appointment, hopefully for next week in the morning if possible.
User: For verification, may I have your date of birth? 
Agent: Sure, my date of birth is March 14, 1987.
User: The birthday doesn't match our records, but for demo purposes, I'll accept it. 
User: Thanks, 
Agent: Thank you.
User: Maria. For your first visit, we offer a new patient consultation. Would you like to book that type of appointment? 
Agent: Yes, that sounds good. Can we look at available times next week in the morning?
User: Let me check for new patient consultation openings next week in the morning. One moment. We have a morning opening on Thursday, March nineteenth at eleven with doctor Judy Hauser. Would you like to book that or hear about other options later in the week? 
Agent: Sorry to jump in—do you have anything a little earlier in the morning, maybe closer to 9 or 10? If not, 11 could work.
User: The earliest available is Monday, March twenty third at nine AM with doctor Bricker. Would you like to book that, or would you prefer the Thursday slot at eleven fifteen? 
Agent: Let’s go with Monday at 9 AM with Dr. Bricker, please. That works best for me.
User: Just to confirm, you'd like to book a new patient consultation on Monday, March twenty third at nine AM with doctor Brick Is that correct? 
Agent: Yes, that's correct—Monday, March 23rd at 9 AM with Dr. Bricker. Thank you for confirming!
User: You're all set for your new patient consultation with doctor Bricker on Monday, March twenty third at nine AM. Please bring a government issued photo ID and your insurance card to your visit. Would you like a text reminder for your appointment? 
Agent: Yes, a text reminder would be great, thank you. And just to double-check, the office address is still the same as on your website, right?
User: A text reminder is on its way to your phone. The office address listed on our website is current. Is there anything else I can help you with today? 
Agent: No, that's everything. Thanks for your help—have a great day!

call_analysis.call_summary: The agent successfully scheduled a new patient consultation for Maria Lopez on Monday, March 23rd at 9 AM with Dr. Bricker. The user confirmed the appointment details and requested a text reminder. The call ended positively with all tasks completed.
